name: Tagus Way to Fátima
name_original: De Santarém a Fátima — Ao longo do Caminho do Tejo
description: Jog the difficult 36.1-mile Caminho do Tejo from Santarém to Fátima, gaining 3554 feet through varied terrain.
region_intro: Jogging around Santarém (São Salvador) offers diverse terrains for runners. The city is situated on a plateau overlooking the Tagus River, providing varied landscapes from elevated viewpoints to expansive river plains. The region features agricultural fields, historic urban areas with cobbled lanes, and accessible routes suitable for different running preferences. Best jogging routes around Santarém (São Salvador) The most popular jogging route is From Santarém to Fátima — Along the Caminho do Tejo, a 36.0 miles (58.0 km) difficult trail that takes 6 hours 35 minutes to complete. This long-distance route follows the historic Caminho do Tejo, offering varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from São Salvador, a moderate 4.4 miles (7.1 km) path. This route provides a convenient loop through the local area, ideal for a quick run. Local runners also love the Santarém loop from São Salvador, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail leading through the historic city center and surrounding areas, often completed in about 1 hour 1 minute. Jogging around Santarém (São Salvador) is defined by plateau views over the Tagus River, agricultural plains, and the historic city center. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy urban runs to more challenging long-distance trails. The routes in Santarém (São Salvador) are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.6 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Santarém (São Salvador)'s varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Santarém (São Salvador)?
A: There are over 30 dedicated running routes around Santarém (São Salvador) listed on komoot, offering a variety of terrains and distances for all levels of runners.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Santarém (São Salvador)?
A: Yes, Santarém (São Salvador) offers several easy routes perfect for beginners. You can find options like the shorter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32151974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from São Salvador&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 7.1 km path, ideal for a quick and accessible run. The city's historic center also provides flatter, urban environments for a gentle jog.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Santarém (São Salvador)?
A: The region offers diverse terrain. You'll find routes on the plateau overlooking the Tagus River, providing varied landscapes and elevated viewpoints. To the east and south, the flat Tagus River Plains offer extensive routes through agricultural fields. The historic city center features cobbled lanes, while some trails lead through woodlands and along riverbanks, such as the peaceful Ribeira da Serta Trail.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails for advanced runners?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2188045413/from-santarem-to-fatima-along-the-caminho-do-tejo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Santarém to Fátima — Along the Caminho do Tejo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 58 km route that follows a historic path, offering varied landscapes and a significant endurance test.

Q: What are some scenic running routes or natural features to explore?
A: Santarém (São Salvador) is rich in scenic beauty. The &lt;strong&gt;Jardins das Portas do Sol&lt;/strong&gt; offer expansive views of the Tagus River valley, making for an inspiring run. You can also find trails along the Tagus River Plains with views of agricultural fields and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2216163" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte D. Luís I of Santarém&lt;/a&gt;. For a more challenging natural experience, the Penedo Furado Trail (a short drive away) features woodlands, streams, and a waterfall.

Q: Can I find running routes that pass through the historic city center?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate the city's rich history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32181293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santarém loop from São Salvador&lt;/a&gt; is a 9.3 km trail that leads through the historic city center, allowing you to jog past cobbled lanes, cultural squares, and Gothic architecture.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32151974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from São Salvador&lt;/a&gt; is a convenient 7.1 km circular path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32179854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Tagus River loop from São Salvador&lt;/a&gt; offers a longer 14.2 km option.

Q: What landmarks or points of interest can I see along the jogging routes?
A: Many routes offer views of significant landmarks. You can run past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2216163" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte D. Luís I of Santarém&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5554761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of Santarém&lt;/a&gt;, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5552902" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jardim das Portas do Sol&lt;/a&gt;. The city also features unique street art like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoopoe Mural by Bordalo II&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Santarém (São Salvador)?
A: The running routes in Santarém (São Salvador) are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.6 stars from over 5 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the scenic views over the Tagus River, and the accessibility of routes for different ability levels.

Q: Are there options for family-friendly jogging routes?
A: Yes, the flatter sections along the Tagus River Plains and some of the shorter, less strenuous loops around São Salvador are suitable for families. The historic city center also provides an engaging urban environment for a family jog, with plenty to see.

Q: Is it possible to find cafes or accommodation near the jogging routes?
A: Yes, particularly in the historic city center and around popular spots like the Jardins das Portas do Sol, you'll find cafes and esplanades perfect for a post-run refreshment. The city offers various accommodation options, many within easy reach of the urban running paths.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Santarém (São Salvador)?
A: Santarém enjoys a Mediterranean climate. Spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for running, with mild weather and beautiful scenery. Summer can be hot, so early mornings or late evenings are recommended during those months.
tips: Just north of Azoia de Baixo, the asphalt gives way to a dirt track that winds along the Ribeira de Cabanas. Here, between open fields and scattered olive trees, nature dominates the landscape. The trail is simple and perfect for running without major obstacles, letting the scenery flow with you.
The Silvas Mill is one of many that dot these fields, recalling times when the wind moved everyday life. The view is stunning, even more so on sunny days, when the landscape lights up.

Near the top of Cabeça Gorda, this viewpoint overlooks the Mira de Aire lagoons and the small town below. Here, a picnic area invites you to take a break: whether to stretch your legs in the sun or take shelter in the shade, depending on your body and the moment.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Caminho do Tejo from Santarém to Fátima?
faqs.answer: The entire route, spanning approximately 58 kilometers, is quite challenging. For an experienced jogger, it typically takes around 6 hours and 35 minutes to complete. This duration can vary significantly based on your fitness level and the pace you maintain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Caminho do Tejo from Santarém to Fátima?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a diverse mix of surfaces. You'll encounter significant paved sections, including asphalt roads and streets, but also substantial unpaved segments, dirt tracks, natural paths, and gravel. For instance, north of Azoia de Baixo, the path transitions to dirt tracks winding through fields and olive trees. The scenery moves through plateaus, mountains, and limestone valleys of the Serras de Aire and Candeeiros.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with young children?
faqs.answer: No, this section of the Caminho do Tejo is generally considered a 'Hard run' and requires a 'very good fitness' level. With its length and considerable elevation gain of over 1,080 meters, it's designed for those with good endurance and is not recommended for beginners or families with young children.

faqs.question: Where does the Caminho do Tejo trail from Santarém to Fátima officially start?
faqs.answer: The trail officially starts in Santarém. While specific trailhead parking isn't detailed, Santarém offers various public parking options. It's advisable to plan your starting point within the city to easily access the initial markers of the Caminho do Tejo.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route from Santarém to Fátima?
faqs.answer: Along the way, you'll experience varied landscapes and points of interest. Notable highlights include the scenic Along the Ribeira de Cabanas, the historic Moinho dos Silvas, and the expansive views from Cabeça Gorda Viewpoint. The route also passes through the unique limestone valleys of the Serras de Aire and Candeeiros.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to jog this route are typically spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is generally milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the potentially wetter, cooler conditions of winter. This makes for a more comfortable and enjoyable experience.

faqs.question: Is the From Santarém to Fátima — Along the Caminho do Tejo dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions against dogs on the Caminho do Tejo, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas, agricultural land, or near wildlife. Ensure your dog is fit enough for the challenging distance and terrain, and carry enough water for both of you.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail or the Parque Natural das Serras de Aire e Candeeiros?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Caminho do Tejo trail itself or to pass through the Parque Natural das Serras de Aire e Candeeiros. The trail is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What unique natural features can I expect to see in the Serras de Aire and Candeeiros?
faqs.answer: The Serras de Aire and Candeeiros are known for their distinctive karst landscape. A unique feature is that rivers and streams are often not visible on the surface, as water flows predominantly underground through a complex network of caves and sinkholes. This creates a rugged and fascinating environment.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is part of the larger Fátima Walking Routes and significantly overlaps with the Caminhos de Fátima (98%) and the Caminho de Tejo (98%). It also briefly intersects with sections of the Caminhos do Mar (Cascais > Fátima > Santiago) (5%), Rota dos Ferreiros (5%), and Rota dos Moinhos (4%).

faqs.question: What should I pack for jogging this challenging trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's length and difficulty, essential items include sufficient water (as surface water sources are scarce), energy snacks, appropriate jogging shoes for varied terrain, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), a first-aid kit, and a fully charged phone with GPS. Layers of clothing are also advisable to adapt to changing weather conditions.
